title: Success Stories: NAILG Secured EB-1A Approval for Expert in Health Informatics Following Successful NIW Approval
content:
Client’s Testimonial:
“Thank you to the WeGreened team for patiently answering all my queries. The team has been tremendously helpful throughout the process, and I am actively referring WeGreened to my colleagues, many of whom have already retained Chen Immigration for their own petitions.”
On January 11 th , 2025, and April 10 th , 2025, we received another EB-2 NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) and EB-1A ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ) approvals for a Robotic Research Fellow in the Field of Health Informatics ( Approval Notice ).
General Field: Health Informatics
Position at the Time of Case Filing: Robotic Research Fellow
Country of Origin: Pakistan
State of Residence at the Time of Filing: Georgia
Approval Notice Date: : January 11 th , 2025 ( NIW ) and April 10 th , 2025 ( EB1A )
Processing Time: 3 months, 22 days ( NIW ), and 14 days ( EB1A ) (Premium Processing Requested)
A leader in health informatics sought NAILG's assistance after successfully securing NIW approval for his work in health informatics. With a focus on improving robotic surgical training, predicting postoperative complications, and reducing disparities in surgical care access, our client’s research has far-reaching implications for advancing healthcare technologies and ensuring equitable medical practices.
His outstanding record includes 34 peer-reviewed journal articles, 7 abstracts, and 2 accepted abstracts. His contributions have been widely recognized, with 127 citations of his research and 812 citations across 13 consortium papers he contributed to.
As an editorial board member and a peer reviewer for 86 journal reviews, his extensive service to the scientific community further establishes him as an authority in his field. His work in improving healthcare access and evaluating TTS in vaccine recipients and the effects of liver transplantation in cancer care has had global relevance in understanding healthcare disparities and improving patient outcomes.
Our client’s accomplishments have been funded by the prestigious Intuitive Surgical Research Foundation , providing further validation of the national and global significance of his work.
An Expert in the Field Noted:
“In summation, [client]’s leadership in health informatics is clearly demonstrated by his cutting-edge research developments on systemic healthcare inequities and outstanding peer review service. Therefore, I do not have any doubts that [client] shall continue to produce additional valuable research, both driving progress in the field and bringing immense benefits to science and research at national and international levels.”
NAILG played a crucial role in guiding our client through the EB-1A petition process, building on his earlier NIW approval. Our team presented a comprehensive petition that highlighted not only his exceptional contributions to health informatics but also the practical applications of his research in robotic surgery and patient care. We ensured the petition clearly demonstrated his exceptional ability and the national importance of his work, particularly in improving healthcare accessibility and systemic inequities. Thanks to NAILG’s expert legal strategy, his EB-1A petition was successfully approved.
--------------------------------------------
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is a U.S. immigration law firm dedicated to representing corporations, research institutions, and individuals from all 50 U.S. states regarding I-140 immigration petitions. We specialize in employment-based immigration petition and have a proven record of high success rate for the categories of: EB2- NIW ( National Interest Waiver ), EB1-A ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ), EB1-B (Outstanding Researcher/Professor) and O-1 ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ).
Our Sixty-four Thousand I-140 Approvals Provide Unprecedented Insight into the USCIS Adjudication Trend
With nearly 64,000 EB-1A , EB-1B , EB-2 NIW and O-1 cases approved, we have first hand information on the manner in which the USCIS adjudicate I-140 cases. As the USCIS has constantly changed its adjudication standards for the EB-1A , EB-1B and EB-2 NIW categories, our firm's huge database of successful cases gives you unprecedented insight to USCIS adjudication trends. We carefully analyze the data for all of our cases and apply the results of our analyses toward giving our clients up-to-date advice and adapting our strategies such that we remain on par with the ever-shifting landscape of immigration law in the U.S. With us, you will always have access to important updates, strategies, and information so that you can make the most informed decisions about your case.
We Have Helped Tens of Thousands of Clients with Credentials and Backgrounds Similar to Yours
With our exceedingly large number of successful petitions, no matter what credentials you have, no matter your background and field of expertise, no matter your visa status or nationality, chances are we have helped hundreds or even thousands of clients just like you. Our clients are usually impressed with how well we understand their research and work. Our insight and understanding stems from the fact that we have handled many cases with elements similar to yours already, and this helps us devise the best strategies for each individual petition.
Vast Majority of Clients Came to Us Because of Referrals
For years, our firm has attracted new clients based solely on word of mouth, recommendations, and the positive collaboration experiences shared with them by their friends and family. We take pride in our reputation and work hard to ensure that we provide a green card application experience that our clients are happy to share with their friends and colleagues. That is how our cumulative total of approved cases grew from 600 in 2013 to nearly 64,000 in 2025.
